stabilization_engine.name: Stabilization Engine
stabilization_engine.description: A compact device that hums with residual power. It was designed to keep something steady — a ship, a platform, a world. In your hands, it steadies your own work.

aspects.text: Still Running After All This Time

stabilization_engine.discovery_text: Among the wreckage of the turbines, you spot a device that's still intact — still humming. The Stabilization Engine. It pulses faintly, as if waiting to be useful again.
stabilization_engine.hint_sensory: something humming through the walls — mechanical, steady, like it never stopped running

salvage_core.name: Salvage Core
salvage_core.description: A compact cylinder of layered metal and crystal, warm to the touch. It was designed to recycle energy from broken systems — pulling power from wreckage and feeding it back into whatever still works. In your hands, it sharpens your awareness of what's useful and what's not.
salvage_core.discovery_text: Wedged between two crumpled cargo containers, a cylinder of layered metal glows faintly from within. The Salvage Core. It hums when you touch it — already scanning, already sorting, already deciding what can be saved.
salvage_core.hint_sensory: a faint warmth between the containers — something recycling power from the wreckage around it

navigators_lodestone.name: Navigator's Lodestone
navigators_lodestone.description: A rough crystal the size of a fist, shot through with veins of pale gold. It tugs gently in your hand, always pulling toward something — a direction, a memory, a place that used to exist. It knows where things are, even when you don't.
navigators_lodestone.discovery_text: On the shattered navigation console, a crystal sits in its mount — the only thing in this room that isn't broken. The Navigator's Lodestone. It tugs toward your hand before you even reach for it, eager to chart a course again.
navigators_lodestone.hint_sensory: a directional pull — faint but insistent, like a compass needle made of light

growth_lattice.name: Growth Lattice
growth_lattice.description: A living framework of crystallized coral that pulses with green light. It resonates with organic growth, amplifying the connection between living things and the void.

aspects.text: The Crystal Has Plans of Its Own

growth_lattice.discovery_text: Nestled among the oldest coral roots, a lattice of living crystal pulses with deep green light. The Growth Lattice. {seed_name} stirs in your awareness — it can feel this thing, and it wants it.
growth_lattice.hint_sensory: a green glow — not bioluminescence, something crystalline, threaded through everything around it

resonance_chime.name: Resonance Chime
resonance_chime.description: A bell-shaped formation of living coral, pale violet and translucent. When struck, it produces a tone that doesn't fade — it spreads outward through every living thing nearby, harmonizing their rhythms. It makes communication effortless, almost involuntary.

aspects.text: Harmony Whether You Want It or Not

resonance_chime.discovery_text: Hanging from a thick coral root, a bell-shaped growth chimes softly in the still air. The Resonance Chime. The sound passes through you — not loud, but deep, like a heartbeat you suddenly share with everything around you.
resonance_chime.hint_sensory: a sustained tone — not mechanical, organic, vibrating through the coral walls like a shared pulse

bioluminous_censer.name: Bioluminous Censer
bioluminous_censer.description: A crystallized light-organ from some deep-void creature, preserved in a lattice of coral. It glows with a cold, steady blue-white light that makes everything around it sharper and clearer. Hidden things become obvious. Shadows lose their secrets.
bioluminous_censer.discovery_text: Among the luminous coral formations, one glow outshines the rest — a crystallized organ suspended in a lattice of pale branches. The Bioluminous Censer. Its light is cold and absolute. In its presence, every crack, every detail, every hidden thing stands out.
bioluminous_censer.hint_sensory: a cold light — brighter than the bioluminescence, sharper, like it's dissolving the shadows on purpose

eliok_house.name: Eliok House
eliok_house.description: A miniature house of impossible craftsmanship, built for beings no larger than your hand. Every surface is carved with scenes of a great tree sheltering a civilization. It radiates warmth and a sense of home so powerful it makes your eyes sting.
eliok_house.discovery_text:
The crystal pillar cracks as you touch it, and the tiny house falls gently into your hands. It's warm. Impossibly warm. Inside, you can see furniture, tapestries, a hearth with a fire that somehow still burns. This is the Eliok House — a home that remembers what home means.

Feed it to {seed_name} for 25 motes — the largest single offering possible.
Or keep it, and it will transform into proper NPC housing on the skerry, complete with a tiny dryad companion who tends the gardens.
eliok_house.hint_sensory: warmth — real warmth, radiating from behind a seal. Something alive in there, or remembering being alive
eliok_house.keep_effect: The Eliok House expands when placed on the skerry, becoming a full-sized dwelling of living wood. A small dryad emerges — a remnant of the Eliok civilization — and begins tending the gardens. All NPC mood bonuses increase. All NPCs gain +1 loyalty. A new aspect appears on the skerry: 'Memory of the Eliok Lives On.'
eliok_house.feed_effect: {seed_name} absorbs the Eliok House and surges with growth. 25 motes flood in. For a moment, you see what {seed_name} sees — a vision of the great tree the Eliok once tended, stretching across an entire world. Then the vision fades, but {seed_name} remembers.

cryogenic_seed_vault.name: Cryogenic Seed Vault
cryogenic_seed_vault.description: A sealed capsule of dark metal, frosted white, containing row upon row of preserved genetic samples. Seeds, spores, cuttings — the biological legacy of a civilization that knew it was dying. The knowledge locked inside could change what grows on the skerry forever.

aspects.text: The Last Gift of a Dying World

cryogenic_seed_vault.discovery_text: In a frost-sealed cabinet, a capsule sits in a cradle of dark metal. The Cryogenic Seed Vault. When you lift it, the frost cracks and falls away, revealing row after row of tiny preserved specimens. A whole world's worth of growing things, sleeping in the ice.
cryogenic_seed_vault.hint_sensory: a sealed chamber — frost thicker here than anywhere else, as if something inside was worth protecting
cryogenic_seed_vault.keep_effect: The Cryogenic Seed Vault's preserved specimens unlock advanced plant varieties for the skerry garden. Rare crops become available. A new aspect appears on the skerry: 'Seeds of a Lost World Take Root.'
cryogenic_seed_vault.feed_effect: {seed_name} absorbs the Cryogenic Seed Vault whole. 18 motes surge in, carrying the genetic memory of an entire world's flora. {seed_name} shudders, branches spreading wider, and for a moment every leaf on the skerry turns a color that doesn't exist in this void.

frost_condenser.name: Frost Condenser
frost_condenser.description: A gauntlet-like device of dark alloy and pale crystal, designed to draw moisture from the air and shape it into ice at will. The cold radiates from it in waves. Worn properly, it can flash-freeze a barrier around you or send a lance of ice at whatever threatens you.

aspects.text: The Cold Answers to You Now

frost_condenser.discovery_text: Mounted on a rack among shattered weapons, a gauntlet of dark alloy still radiates cold. The Frost Condenser. When you lift it, ice crystallizes across your forearm in an intricate lattice — not painful, protective. The gauntlet knows what it's for.
frost_condenser.hint_sensory: an intense cold — focused, deliberate, radiating from a weapons rack like it's guarding something
frost_condenser.keep_effect: The Frost Condenser sheathes your arm in reactive ice armor. In combat, it absorbs the first hit each encounter. Outside combat, it can shape ice for construction and repair. A new aspect appears: 'Armed With Winter.'
frost_condenser.feed_effect: {seed_name} absorbs the Frost Condenser and the cold hits like a wall. 20 motes pour in, and for a moment {seed_name}'s tendrils crystallize into glass-clear ice before thawing back to living green. The cold is gone, but {seed_name} remembers the shape of it.

silver_slippers.name: Silver Slippers
silver_slippers.description: A pair of delicate silver slippers, impossibly light, threaded with filaments that shimmer like starlight. They hum faintly when you hold them, as if remembering a road that no longer exists.
silver_slippers.discovery_text: Silver slippers, barely tarnished, sitting on the bare ground as if someone set them down and walked away. They catch the light in ways the light shouldn't bend.
silver_slippers.hint_sensory: a faint shimmer — like starlight caught in something solid, humming with a road that isn't there

bloom_catalyst.name: Bloom Catalyst
bloom_catalyst.description: A crystallized flower bud that pulses with deep green energy. When held near dormant plants, they erupt into instant growth — flowers, fruit, seeds, all at once. The crystal dims slightly each time it's used.
bloom_catalyst.discovery_text: At the base of the crystallized plant, a single bud glows with concentrated life. The Bloom Catalyst. When you pick it up, every plant in the chamber shivers.
bloom_catalyst.hint_sensory: concentrated life — a green pulse, like a heartbeat, deep inside the overgrowth

mycorrhizal_nexus.name: Mycorrhizal Nexus
mycorrhizal_nexus.description: A dense knot of fungal tissue, pale white and faintly luminous, threaded with gossamer-thin filaments that spread outward in every direction. It's the hub of an underground network — a nervous system for the entire biodome. Holding it, you feel the faint emotional echoes of every living thing it once connected.
mycorrhizal_nexus.discovery_text: Beneath the nursery's topsoil, a pale knot of fungal tissue glows faintly. The Mycorrhizal Nexus. When you pull it free, gossamer threads trail from it like severed nerves, and for a moment you feel everything in the biodome — the hunger of roots, the patience of spores, the slow grief of soil.
mycorrhizal_nexus.hint_sensory: a pale glow beneath the soil — fungal, spreading, like a buried nervous system still firing signals

chloroplast_prism.name: Chloroplast Prism
chloroplast_prism.description: A translucent green crystal the size of a plum, dense with swirling internal light. It's a concentrated fragment of photosynthetic energy — the biodome's growth engine, crystallized into a single point. Near it, plants lean closer. Away from it, they wilt.
chloroplast_prism.discovery_text: Among the overgrown hydroponics racks, a green crystal catches light that isn't there. The Chloroplast Prism. Every vine in the greenhouse bends toward it. When you pick it up, the nearest plants visibly droop, as if you've taken their sun.
chloroplast_prism.hint_sensory: green light — not reflected, generated, pulsing from somewhere in the overgrowth like a tiny captive sun

red_clown_nose.name: Red Clown Nose
red_clown_nose.description: A round, squishy red nose. It looks absurd. It feels absurd. But when you hold it, something about it makes you feel less conspicuous, less threatening — less worth envying.
red_clown_nose.discovery_text: A red clown nose, sitting on the ground like a joke with no punchline. It's perfectly round, impossibly vivid against the dull soil.
red_clown_nose.hint_sensory: something impossibly vivid — bright red, round, completely out of place
